data.title: Banco Santander-Chile reports additional capital requirement of 25 basis points
data.description:
Banco Santander-Chile (NYSE:BSAC) said in a filing on Monday that the Financial Market Commission, or CMF, has decided to establish an additional capital requirement of 25 basis points.

The measure is to be applied progressively, with 50% of the charge to be completed by June 30.

The Banco Santander (SAN) (OTCPK:BCDRF) unit said it already had capital buffers above the regulatory minimums, and compliance with this requirement does not imply the need for new capital contributions.

The charge will be absorbed with resources already available within the bank's effective equity, said Banco Santander-Chile.

data.title: Charter Communications most shorted S&P 500 communications stock in March; Alphabet least shorted
data.description:
Bets against communication services stocks rose in March compared to the end of February, with Charter Communications (CHTR) being the most shorted stock in the sector and Google-parent Alphabet (GOOG) taking the spot for the least shorted.

Average short interest across S&P 500 communications services stocks was 1.47% of shares float in end-March, up from 1.42% at the end of February.

The S&P 500’s communications services sector (NYSEARCA:XLC) is down over 4% so far this year, compared to the over 8% fall in the broader market.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

 

 

Meta Platforms Class A (META), Alphabet Class A (GOOGL) and Netflix (NFLX), the three largest contributors to the index, had short interest of 1.24%, 1.16% and 1.9%, respectively.

Charter Communications had 12.25 million shares sold short as of March 31, or 13.05% of shares float.

Omnicom Group (OMC) was the second most shorted stock at 11.68% of shares float, followed by Paramount Global (PARA) and Live Nation Entertainment (LYV), with short interest of 11.11% and 8.85%, respectively.

Alphabet was preceded by Meta Platforms (META), which had 27.19 million shares sold.

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

 

 

Media was the most shorted industry within the sector, with 3.32% short interest as of end-March, up from 3.34% during end-February.

Entertainment stocks took the second spot with 2.91% short interest as of March 31, compared to 2.48% at February-end.

Interactive Media and Services, which has stocks like Alphabet and Meta Platforms, was the least shorted industry in the sector with 0.59% short interest as of March.

data.title: White House has received at least 10 'amazing offers' for trade deals: NEC's Hassett
data.description:
The White House has received at least 10 “amazing offers” for trade deals, and a recession is “100% not” going to happen after President Donald Trump imposed tariffs on imports from many of the country’s trading partners, National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett said Monday.

"We've got everybody in the trade team and even deputies of people in the trade team talking to just about everybody on Earth,” Hassett said in an interview with Fox Business. “And I think that we've got more than 10 deals where there's very good, amazing offers made to the U.S. that USTR Trade Representative Jamieson Greer and [Commerce Secretary] Howard Lutnick and the rest of our trade team of the president are stewing over whether those deals are good enough.”

Hassett said Greer and Lutnick are considering if the offers are “good enough.” He did not name any countries. 

“The one question that I have open in my mind is, should we go one at a time, or should we announce a bundle all at once?” Hassett said. 

He added that there a recession is “100% not” going to happen, 

Hassett said corporate executives told him that companies are pushing to return their foreign operations to the U.S., and consumers are buying imported goods since they are concerned about future tariffs. 

“So everything’s through the roof, anecdotally,” he said, adding that March’s jobs report was “really, really good.”

data.title: OpenAI releases new GPT-4.1 models at lower costs for developers
data.description:
OpenAI launched its latest series of flagship models, GPT-4.1, which were trained specifically for developers, and are expected to be 26% less expensive to run than GPT-4o.

"Today, we are excited to announce GPT-4.1, which is a family of models in the API that were trained just for developers," said OpenAI's chief product officer Kevin Weil, in a livestream today. "It's three models, GPT-4.1, GPT 4.1 mini and for the first time, GPT-4.1 Nano, which is our smallest, fastest and cheapest model ever ... These models are better than GPT-4o on just about every dimension. They even meet or beat 4.5 in a bunch of key ways. They are great at coding, great at complex instruction following, fantastic at building agents."

In an example, a user prompts GPT-4.1 to produce a flash card application to assist in learning Hindi. GPT-4.1 quickly created an animated flash card set.

The new models, including mini and Nano, can use context length of up to 1M tokens as compared to earlier models that could utilize 128,000 tokens.

Pricing for the new models are 26% cheaper than GPT-4o. Nano will become OpenAI's cheapest model at $0.12 blended per million tokens.

Earlier this month, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said it will be a few months before the Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) company launches GPT-5, as it works to acquire enough GPU capacity to support the demand.

data.title: Pagaya prices ~$300M auto ABS transaction demonstrating strong investor confidence
data.description:
• Pagaya Technologies (NASDAQ:PGY) has announced the pricing of a new $295M AA-rated auto loan ABS transaction. Amid market volatility, investors continue to seek high-quality assets with attractive yields.
  • Pagaya’s seasoned RPM shelf, now in its sixth year, continues to be a trusted source of consistent execution and performance.
  • “Pagaya’s RPM Shelf has developed a track record of delivering strong returns for its investors. These are the ideal market dislocations where investors can take calculated risk,” said Gal Krubiner, Pagaya’s CEO and Co-Founder.
  • Pagaya’s ability to efficiently execute its latest auto ABS deal, reflects continued strong capital markets demand for Pagaya’s assets.
  • “In an increasingly competitive capital markets environment, our Auto ABS program showcases that demand for and investor confidence in our auto assets remains high,” said Sahil Chandiramani, Pagaya’s Head of Capital Markets.

data.title: Green stock exchange gets go-ahead from SEC
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Stock Exchange approved the Green Impact Exchange's ("GIX") Form 1 application, clearing the way for a stock market focused on the $35T sustainability economy, the company said on Monday.

The new exchange, which expects to start trading in early 2026, will be a listings and trading venue that's focused on sustainability, serving public companies and investors, it said.

GIX’s trading platform will be powered by technology from exchange operator MEMX, and will offer non-tiered, competitive liquidity and quoting programs for members and market makers. GIX will also be part of the National Market System ("NMS"), ensuring best execution for all trades directed or routed to GIX, it added.

"Climate risk is business risk. It’s that simple," said GIX President and co-founder Charles Dolan. "U.S. investors and companies are continuing to pursue sustainability because it makes financial and competitive sense. Public markets like GIX have a pivotal role to play in connecting sustainable investors with companies that understand that."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Evercore gives a rundown on internet stocks ahead of Q1 report
data.description:
Analysts at Evercore think investors are in the midst of a bear market for internet stocks.

They say the average large-cap internet stock is off close to 20% vs. February peak levels and believe over 70% of them are trading within 20% of their 3-year trough valuation levels. In terms of valuation setup, the risk/reward for them is now intrinsically attractive.

The research firm said in the wake of the market selloff, with the exception of Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Shopify (NASDAQ:SHOP), internet stocks have very limited direct exposure to tariff risk, but the cyclical risk to recession is widespread.

Evercore said its March quarter preview work suggests that results should be "reasonably solid" but is increasingly cautious for the June quarter.

Top four large-cap internet longs: Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), Uber (NYSE:UBER), Meta (NASDAQ:META), and Google (NASDAQ:GOOGL).

Stocks with the most EPS risk for Q1: Expedia (NASDAQ:EXPE), Airbnb (NASDAQ:ABNB), and Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD).

Stocks with the least EPS risk for Q1: Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX), Spotify (NYSE:SPOT), and Uber (NYSE:UBER).

data.title: GCM Grosvenor, Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Bank partner for private markets expansion
data.description:
Alternative asset manager GCM Grosvenor (NASDAQ:GCMG) announced on Monday a strategic partnership with the Tokyo-based Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Bank to expand private markets offerings.

The collaboration aims to expand SuMi TRUST's initiatives in private markets assets, expand the distribution of GCM Grosvenor's private market investment products to investors in Japan, and jointly develop private markets investment products focused on Japanese markets for distribution to SuMi TRUST clients in Japan as well as to GCM Grosvenor clients globally.

SuMi TRUST is set to acquire $50M of newly issued class A shares of GCM Grosvenor, and committed $100M to Elevate, GCM Grosvenor's private equity seeding strategy.

The two companies target at least an additional $1.5B of AUM from the partnership by 2030.

GCM Grosvenor and Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Bank have collaborated since 2016 across private equity, credit, and infrastructure strategies.

data.title: LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton Q1 sales dip 3% weighing on luxury names
data.description:
Led by declining sales in its Wine & Spirits and Fashion & Leather Goods categories, French luxury giant LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton’s (OTCPK:LVMUY) (OTCPK:LVMHF) total organic sales in the first quarter were down 3%, reflecting a slowdown in consumer spending on high-end merchandise. Wall Street expected sales to improve 1.1%.

The results are weighing on shares of competitors in the luxury space with Kering SA (OTCPK:PPRUY) surrendering early gains to trade 6% in the red, L'Oréal S.A. (OTCPK:LRLCF) (OTCPK:LRLCY) down fractionally, Hermes (OTCPK:HESAY) down 1%, and Prada S.p.A. (OTCPK:PRDSY) down 3%. U.S-listed companies are also down in sympathy with Capri (CPRI), Tapestry (TPR), and Signet (SIG) all lower.

Accounting for nearly half of all sales, revenue in the company’s fashion and leather goods segment fell by 5%. And although champagne sales were slightly lower from a year ago, weak sales of cognac in China and the U.S. resulted in a 9% drop in the wine and spirits category.

By region, European sales rose but were down in the U.S. amid a volatile economic environment. Japanese sales were down as well, while the rest of Asia, including China, saw sales trends similar to those in 2024.

“In a disrupted geopolitical and economic environment, LVMH remains both vigilant and confident at the start of the year. The Group remains focused on the development of its brands, driven by a sustained policy of innovation and investment as well as by a constant quest for quality in its products, their desirability and their selective distribution,” the Paris-based conglomerate said in a statement.

The disappointing performance of its alcohol business fueled rumors that the parent company was considering a sale of its Moët Hennessy business. However, according to CFO Cecile Cabanis, reports of a split between Louis Vuitton and Moët Hennessy are “very wrong.”

Shares of LVMH Moët Hennessy (OTCPK:LVMUY) are more than 6% lower in U.S. trading.

data.title: Dover rated Equal-weight in new coverage at Morgan Stanley on growth doubts
data.description:
Dover (NYSE:DOV) on Monday was rated Equal-weight in new research coverage by analysts at financial-services firm Morgan Stanley. They said the maker of industrial equipment has promising growth catalysts, but they questioned whether higher growth beyond next year is sustainable.

“Dover’s (NYSE:DOV) organic growth should outpace peers in 2025 as it laps acute headwinds, but we are yet to see signs that mid-single-digit organic growth is durable,” Christopher Snyder, research analyst at Morgan Stanley, said in an April 14 report to clients.

Dover has five main operating segments, with corporate brands such as Microwave Products Group, Vehicle Service Group, OK International and Tulsa Winch.

Portfolio repositioning lifts growth outlook

As a diversified global manufacturer, Dover (DOV) historically has delivered modest organic growth — just a 2% compound annual rate over the past decade. But recent strategic moves to “high-grade” the portfolio — emphasizing sectors like biopharma and clean energy — have set the stage for better performance.

Snyder noted that consensus estimates already reflect this expected acceleration, projecting 4% organic growth from 2025 to 2027, nearly triple the company’s long-term average. While Morgan Stanley also models a step-up in 2025, helped by the easing of 300 to 400 basis points of headwinds, the firm remains cautious about what happens once those tailwinds fade.

“If growth ultimately fades back to low single digits on normalizing comps, it will be difficult for the equity to re-rate,” Snyder warned.

Margins, M&A are upside levers

Despite reservations about growth durability, the analysts see the possibility for Dover (DOV) to expand margins and deploy capital effectively. Under CEO Rich Tobin, who took the helm in 2018, the company has shifted from divestitures to acquisitions — armed with $2 billion in cash and generating $1 billion in annual free cash flow.

“As Dover (DOV) shifts from net seller to net buyer, earnings per share and ebitda will grow at a faster rate — potentially acting as a catalyst for upward revisions and multiple expansion,” Snyder said.

Dover (DOV) has also demonstrated solid margin execution, expanding by about 60 basis points annually in the past five years despite mix and volume pressures. With an increasingly lean cost structure and productivity gains, the company could deliver incremental margins above the 25%–35% target shared during its last investor day.

Valuation and rating outlook

Morgan Stanley’s $185 price target is based on a valuation of 18 times next-twelve-month EPS for 2026–2027, a roughly 10% discount to the S&P 500 (SP500) and 20% discount to peers. While that’s slightly tighter than historical levels, it reflects confidence in Dover’s improved portfolio mix and a belief that end markets are near a cyclical trough.

“We think a further re-rating would require more conviction in mid-single-digit organic durability,” Snyder added.

What could change

Bullish scenario: Morgan Stanley would consider turning more positive on Dover (DOV) if signs emerge of sustained strength in higher-margin growth categories — such as thermal connectors, biopharma and carbon dioxide systems — or if the company demonstrates a robust and executable M&A pipeline.

Bearish scenario: Conversely, a more negative outlook could emerge if key growth platforms stall, if pricing power is eroded by Trump 2.0 tariffs, or if Dover’s (DOV) capital deployment underwhelms expectations.

In the meantime, Snyder said Dover (DOV) is well-positioned but fairly valued, with growth expectations high and the burden now on execution.
